text: But he gave none of the wagons or oxen to the Kohathite division, since they were required to carry the sacred objects of the Tabernacle on their shoulders.

content:
Continuing from yesterday, we have already seen how every part of the Ark of the Covenant pointed to Christ; the acacia wood, the gold, the stone tablets of the Law He perfectly fulfilled, the budded rod of His resurrection and living priesthood, and the golden jar of manna. Today we move forward to the spiritual meaning of the Kohathites carrying the Ark. What does it mean that the congregation; you and I, carry this on our shoulders today?

Do you remember the meaning of the name Kohath? We studied it earlier. Kohath means assembly, congregation, called together; one who obeys a call, an ally, a convocation. The Kohathites were the assembled, called-together people of God. And it was this assembled people; not angels, not perfect individuals; who were entrusted with carrying the holiest objects in the universe on their shoulders. This is still the way God works. The congregation, the called-together people of the new covenant, are the ones entrusted with carrying the holy things of God through the wilderness of this world.

The Ark was not merely a symbol of God’s presence. It was the actual dwelling place of His glory among His people. Wherever the Ark went, God’s glory went. When the Ark crossed the Jordan River, the waters stopped flowing and the people crossed on dry ground (Joshua 3:15-17). When the Ark came to rest at the house of Obed-edom, Yahweh blessed that entire household for three months (2 Samuel 6:11). The presence of the Ark changed the atmosphere of every place it entered. It did not need to do anything else. Its presence was enough.

In the new covenant, the presence of God no longer dwells inside a gold chest. It dwells inside people. “For we are the temple of the living God; as God said, ‘I will dwell in them and walk among them” (2 Corinthians 6:16). “Christ in you, the hope of glory” (Colossians 1:27). We are the new Ark. When a new creation believer walks into a room, the presence of God enters that room. When we go into a home, a hospital, a workplace, a difficult situation; the Ark goes there. The glory that once rested between the cherubim on the Mercy Seat now rests in and moves through the assembled congregation of the redeemed. This is not a small thing. This is the most extraordinary truth about the life of a believer.

This is why the world is affected when the church is truly the church.
Not because of our programmes, our buildings, our eloquence, or our strategies; but because of what we carry inside us. Wherever the Ark rested, things changed. Wherever the genuine presence of Christ rests in His people, things still change. We do not need to manufacture results. We need to carry the presence faithfully. The Ark did not strive to part the Jordan. It simply arrived, and the waters obeyed.

The two stone tablets inside the Ark tell a story in themselves. The first set of tablets was broken. Moses came down from the mountain, saw Israel worshipping the golden calf, and threw the tablets to the ground (Exodus 32:19). Those broken tablets speak of every man and woman who has ever tried to keep God’s Law in their own strength and failed. Not one person born of Adam has kept it fully. The breaking of the first tablets is the story of all of us. But God gave a second set. Whole. Complete. Preserved inside the Ark and carried through the wilderness. The second tablets speak of Christ, who never broke a single commandment; who fulfilled the Law perfectly, from the inside out, from the heart, with joy. “I delight to do Your will, O my God; Your Law is within my heart” (Psalm 40:8).

As new creation believers, we do not carry the Law as a burden of condemnation. Romans 8:1 says clearly: “There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.” We carry the completed righteousness of Christ; the testimony that the Law has been fully met, the debt has been fully paid, the broken tablets have been answered by the One who never broke even one. We carry this message to people who are crushed under guilt, striving to be good enough, trying to earn what can never be earned.
We carry the good news that the Law has already been satisfied in Christ.

But we also carry the Law as it was always meant to be; not written on cold stone from the outside, but written on living hearts from the inside. This is what Yahweh promised through the prophet Ezekiel *(Though it is for Israel, it can still be applied for us)*: “I will put my Spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes” (Ezekiel 36:27). The new creation believer carries a changed nature; a heart that now genuinely desires holiness, a life being slowly and steadily conformed to the image of Christ. The world watches and sees something it cannot fully explain: people for whom the commands of God are not burdensome chains but the shape of a life being set free. We carry that testimony. We carry the truth of what God requires and what God Himself provides, because we could never have provided it on our own.

Aaron’s budded rod inside the Ark was the sign that God brings dead things back to life. As new creation believers, we carry the testimony of the resurrection everywhere we go. We do not carry a memory of a great teacher who once lived and died. We carry the message; and the living reality, of a risen Christ who conquered death and is alive this very moment. The rod was cut off, dead, without root or soil or water. It was placed in darkness before the presence of God. In the morning it was covered in buds, blossoms, and ripe almonds. This is exactly what happened to Christ. Cut off from the land of the living (Isaiah 53:8), placed in the tomb, and on the third morning; resurrection life, as impossible and as glorious as almonds on a dead stick.

Romans 8:11 tells us: “If the Spirit of Him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, He who raised Christ Jesus from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through His Spirit who dwells in you.” The very same resurrection power that made a dead stick bloom overnight in the presence of God is the power living inside every believer right now. We carry it on our shoulders into dead marriages, broken families, communities with no hope left, and people who have stopped believing that anything can change. We carry a single word into every dead place: this is not final. The rod bloomed. The tomb is empty. Nothing is too dead for God.

And we must remember; every new creation believer is themselves a testimony of resurrection. We were dead in our sins and trespasses (Ephesians 2:1).
God made us alive. Every person who has been born again is a dead rod that budded. We carry the testimony not only in our words but in our very existence. We are walking proof that God raises dead things to life. The world does not need more arguments about resurrection. It needs to see people whose lives have been raised.

We have seen that we carry the presence of God, the fulfilled righteousness of Christ, and the power of resurrection on our shoulders. But there is still more inside the Ark that we are carrying. Inside that gold chest was also a golden jar of manna; bread that could not rot, bread that came down from heaven; and on top of the Ark sat the Mercy Seat, the one place in all the universe where sinful man could meet a holy God. What does it mean that we carry these on our shoulders today? And what is the solemn warning the Ark gives to everyone who dares to carry it? We will continue tomorrow.

title: Prayer
text:
You, Yahweh, are great and greatly to be praised; Your greatness is unsearchable (Psalm 145:3). Generation after generation shall declare Your works, and we will proclaim Your mighty acts. You are gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love. We thank You that You have not left us empty-handed in this world. You have placed Your own presence inside us, Your righteousness upon us, and the power of resurrection within us. Forgive us for the days we walked as though we carried nothing, as though Your presence made no difference, as though the resurrection were only a doctrine and not a living power. Open our eyes to the weight of what is on our shoulders. Help us carry it into every place we go today; knowing that wherever the Ark went, things changed, and that You have not changed.
In Christ’s name, amen.
